title: ADCS Attacks
description: This module focuses on Active Directory Certificate Services (ADCS) and its potential misconfigurations. Misconfigurations can allow horizontal and vertical privilege escalation and lateral movement within a domain. It covers all domain escalation scenarios disclosed by SpecterOps and later discoveries, showing how to exploit them from both Windows and Linux systems. Each topic includes hands-on exercises, and the module concludes with a practical skills assessment. Prerequisites include knowledge of Active Directory Enumeration & Attacks and Kerberos Attacks.

title: Footprinting
description: Footprinting (HTB Academy) covers the enumeration of services like FTP, SMB, SQL, MSSQL, IMAP/POP3, DNS, NFS, and SMTP, along with hands-on labs to reinforce each technique.

title: File Transfers
description: File Transfers (HTB Academy) is a hands-on module covering various file transfer methods and the risks of detection during transfers.

title: Shells & Payloads
description: Shells & Payloads (HTB Academy) covers crafting payloads tailored to target architecture, understanding bind vs reverse shells, experimenting with various shell types (bash, cmd, PowerShell, web shells), and using Metasploit for exploitation through hands-on labs.

title: Password Attacks
description: Password Attacks (HTB Academy) covers password cracking techniques, hash cracking tools (John, Hashcat), custom wordlist creation, remote password attacks, credential extraction from Windows/Linux systems and network traffic, and lateral movement techniques like Pass-the-Hash, Pass-the-Ticket, and Pass-the-Certificate.

title: Attacking Common Services
description: Attacking Common Services (HTB Academy) focuses on identifying, analyzing, and exploiting commonly exposed services such as FTP, SSH, SMB, RDP, and Telnet. It covers misconfiguration discovery, service-specific vulnerabilities, and practical exploitation techniques including password-based attacks.

title: Pivoting, Tunneling, and Port Forwarding
description: Pivoting, Tunneling, and Port Forwarding (HTB Academy) covers techniques for accessing internal networks through compromised hosts, including pivoting, port forwarding, and tunneling. The module includes hands-on labs using tools like SSH, SOCKS proxies, Chisel, and Metasploit to establish pivot infrastructure.

title: Active Directory Enumeration & Attacks
description: Active Directory Enumeration & Attacks (HTB Academy) focuses on enumerating and attacking Active Directory in both modern and legacy enterprise environments. It covers BloodHound and Kerbrute usage, SMB Null sessions, password spraying, ACL abuse, domain trust exploitation, Kerberoasting, and privilege escalation. The module also includes native tool abuse and AD hardening techniques.

title: Using Web Proxies
description: Using Web Proxies (HTB Academy) teaches how to effectively use tools like Burp Suite and ZAP to intercept, analyze, and manipulate web traffic, discover hidden features, perform fuzzing, and identify vulnerabilities through passive and active scanning. The module also includes proxying traffic from other tools and extending functionality via plugins.

title: Attacking Web Applications with Ffuf
description: Attacking Web Applications with Ffuf (HTB Academy) focuses on discovering hidden directories, pages, virtual hosts, and parameters within web applications. It covers using Ffuf for directory brute-forcing, vhost enumeration, PHP parameter fuzzing, and testing parameter values through hands-on practice.

title: Login Brute Forcing
description: Login Brute Forcing (HTB Academy) covers systematic brute-force attacks against HTTP authentication, web login forms, and remote services like SSH and FTP. Through hands-on exercises using tools like Hydra and Medusa, it explores dictionary and hybrid attacks, default credentials, custom wordlist creation, and the exploitation of weak authentication mechanisms.

title: Vulnerability Assessment
description: Vulnerability Assessment (HTB Academy) covers security assessments, vulnerability scoring and reporting using tools like Nessus and OpenVAS through hands-on exercises.

title: Penetration Testing Process
description: Penetration Testing Process (HTB Academy) covers the full lifecycle of a penetration test, including target scoping, information gathering, vulnerability analysis, exploitation, persistence, and reporting.

title: Network Enumeration with Nmap
description: Network Enumeration with Nmap (HTB Academy) focuses on discovering target systems, identifying services, and bypassing security measures using Nmap.

title: SQL Injection Fundamentals
description:
Database Management systems offer faster storage and retrieval of data in comparison to traditional file storage. This makes them the medium of choice for storing data such as credentials, posts, and comments used by web applications. However, improperly implemented SQL logic can be dangerous and can lead to authentication bypass, information disclosure, remote code execution, and total server compromise.

Applications implement code to create SQL queries to interact with databases. These queries can incorporate user input, which may not be properly sanitized. This leads to the creation of queries that cause unintended actions other than what the developer expected.

This module aims to develop the skills necessary to identify and exploit SQL injection vulnerabilities, mainly for MySQL databases, and as an intro to all other types of SQL injections.

In this module, we will cover the following topics:

    Basics of databases and their different types
    Basics of SQL and MySQL
    Basic statements and operators in MySQL and how to use them
    What are SQL injections, and how can we use them
    Use SQL injections to subvert the web application logic and bypass authentication
    Use UNION SQL injections to dump data from different tables and databases within the DBMS
    Use SQL injections to read files of the back-end server
    Use SQL injections to write a web shell on the back-end server and gain remote control over it
    How to mitigate such SQL injections and patch your code
